description: FastAPI service health check endpoint returns 200 OK
steps: Step 1: Start FastAPI service on port 8000
Step 2: Navigate to http://localhost:8000/health using puppeteer
Step 3: Verify response status is 200 OK
Step 4: Verify response contains health status indicator

description: Create new endpoint via REST API POST /endpoints
steps: Step 1: Navigate to http://localhost:8000/docs using puppeteer
Step 2: Use puppeteer_evaluate to make fetch() POST to /endpoints with test endpoint data
Step 3: Verify response status is 201 Created
Step 4: Verify response contains endpoint id and all submitted fields
Step 5: Query database to confirm endpoint was persisted

description: Retrieve all endpoints via GET /endpoints
steps: Step 1: Create 3 test endpoints via POST /endpoints
Step 2: Use puppeteer_evaluate to make fetch() GET to /endpoints
Step 3: Verify response is array with 3 endpoints
Step 4: Verify each endpoint has required fields: id, name, url, method, environment

description: Update endpoint configuration via PUT /endpoints/:id
steps: Step 1: Create test endpoint with check_interval=300
Step 2: Use puppeteer_evaluate to PUT /endpoints/:id with check_interval=600
Step 3: Verify response status is 200 OK
Step 4: GET /endpoints/:id and verify check_interval updated to 600

description: Delete endpoint via DELETE /endpoints/:id
steps: Step 1: Create test endpoint and record its id
Step 2: Use puppeteer_evaluate to DELETE /endpoints/:id
Step 3: Verify response status is 204 No Content or 200 OK
Step 4: GET /endpoints/:id and verify 404 Not Found

description: Manual check trigger via POST /endpoints/:id/check
steps: Step 1: Create endpoint pointing to known good URL (e.g., https://httpbin.org/status/200)
Step 2: Use puppeteer_evaluate to POST /endpoints/:id/check
Step 3: Verify response contains check result with passed=true
Step 4: Query checks table and verify new check row was inserted

description: Toggle endpoint enable/disable via PUT /endpoints/:id/toggle
steps: Step 1: Create enabled endpoint (enabled=true)
Step 2: Use puppeteer_evaluate to PUT /endpoints/:id/toggle
Step 3: Verify endpoint.enabled is now false
Step 4: Toggle again and verify enabled=true

description: Scheduled health check executes at configured interval and saves result to database
steps: Step 1: Create endpoint with check_interval=60 seconds pointing to https://httpbin.org/status/200
Step 2: Start APScheduler and wait 65 seconds
Step 3: Query checks table for endpoint_id
Step 4: Verify at least 1 check row exists with passed=true, status_code=200, response_time>0

description: Failed check recorded when endpoint returns non-200 status
steps: Step 1: Create endpoint pointing to https://httpbin.org/status/500
Step 2: Trigger manual check via POST /endpoints/:id/check
Step 3: Verify check result has passed=false
Step 4: Verify error_message mentions status code 500

description: Check timeout recorded when endpoint exceeds timeout_ms
steps: Step 1: Create endpoint with timeout_ms=100 pointing to https://httpbin.org/delay/5
Step 2: Trigger manual check
Step 3: Verify check result has passed=false
Step 4: Verify error_message contains 'timeout' or 'TimeoutError'

description: Incident opened after 3 consecutive check failures
steps: Step 1: Create endpoint pointing to failing URL (https://httpbin.org/status/503)
Step 2: Trigger 3 manual checks in sequence
Step 3: Query incidents table for endpoint_id
Step 4: Verify 1 incident exists with started_at set, resolved_at=null
Step 5: Verify incident.failure_count >= 3

description: Incident auto-closed after 2 consecutive passing checks
steps: Step 1: Create endpoint, trigger 3 failures to open incident
Step 2: Update endpoint URL to passing endpoint (https://httpbin.org/status/200)
Step 3: Trigger 2 manual passing checks
Step 4: Query incidents table and verify incident.resolved_at is now set
Step 5: Verify duration_mins is calculated correctly

description: Claude AI incident report generation on incident open
steps: Step 1: Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable or use /tmp/api-key
Step 2: Create endpoint and trigger incident (3 failures)
Step 3: Wait up to 15 seconds for async Claude report generation
Step 4: Query incidents table and verify claude_report field is populated
Step 5: Verify report contains plain-English summary and root causes

description: Manual incident reanalysis via POST /incidents/:id/reanalyze
steps: Step 1: Open incident with Claude report
Step 2: Clear claude_report field in database
Step 3: Use puppeteer_evaluate to POST /incidents/:id/reanalyze
Step 4: Wait for Claude API response
Step 5: Verify claude_report is repopulated with fresh analysis

description: SLA uptime calculation for 24h/7d/30d windows
steps: Step 1: Create endpoint and insert 100 check rows (80 passed, 20 failed) over 24h
Step 2: Use puppeteer_evaluate to GET /sla/:endpoint_id
Step 3: Verify uptime_24h = 80.0%
Step 4: Verify SLA target comparison (e.g., below 99.9% target)

description: SLA CSV export via GET /sla/:endpoint_id/export
steps: Step 1: Create endpoint with historical check data
Step 2: Use puppeteer_evaluate to GET /sla/:endpoint_id/export
Step 3: Verify response Content-Type is text/csv
Step 4: Verify CSV contains columns: date, uptime_24h, uptime_7d, uptime_30d

description: YAML endpoint export via GET /endpoints/export
steps: Step 1: Create 3 test endpoints with different configurations
Step 2: Use puppeteer_evaluate to GET /endpoints/export
Step 3: Verify response is valid YAML format
Step 4: Verify YAML contains all 3 endpoints with name, url, method, environment fields

description: YAML endpoint import via POST /endpoints/import
steps: Step 1: Create YAML file with 2 endpoint definitions
Step 2: Use puppeteer_evaluate to POST /endpoints/import with YAML content
Step 3: Verify response indicates 2 endpoints imported
Step 4: GET /endpoints and verify the 2 new endpoints exist in database

description: Email alert sent on incident open
steps: Step 1: Configure alert_config for endpoint with channel=email, on_incident=true
Step 2: Trigger incident (3 consecutive failures)
Step 3: Verify alerter.send_email() was called with incident details
Step 4: Check alert log table for email send record with timestamp

description: Slack webhook alert sent on incident open
steps: Step 1: Configure alert_config with channel=slack, target=webhook_url
Step 2: Trigger incident
Step 3: Verify httpx POST to Slack webhook was made
Step 4: Verify alert log contains Slack send record with message preview

description: Desktop notification sent on incident open
steps: Step 1: Configure alert_config with channel=desktop
Step 2: Trigger incident
Step 3: Verify plyer.notification.notify() was called
Step 4: Verify alert log contains desktop notification record

description: Alert cooldown prevents duplicate alerts within 15 minutes
steps: Step 1: Configure alert with cooldown_mins=15
Step 2: Trigger incident and verify first alert sent (last_sent_at recorded)
Step 3: Trigger second incident for same endpoint within 10 minutes
Step 4: Verify second alert is NOT sent
Step 5: Wait 15+ minutes and trigger third incident, verify alert IS sent

description: Incident acknowledgment via PUT /incidents/:id/acknowledge
steps: Step 1: Create incident
Step 2: Use puppeteer_evaluate to PUT /incidents/:id/acknowledge
Step 3: Verify incident.acknowledged = true in database
Step 4: Verify response confirms acknowledgment timestamp

description: Streamlit dashboard displays endpoint status grid on http://localhost:8501
steps: Step 1: Create 6 endpoints (2 dev, 2 staging, 2 production)
Step 2: Navigate to http://localhost:8501 using puppeteer_navigate
Step 3: Take screenshot with puppeteer_screenshot
Step 4: Verify page shows environment tabs: All, Dev, Staging, Production
Step 5: Verify status cards are visible for all 6 endpoints
Step 6: Verify summary metrics row shows total/UP/degraded/down counts

description: Streamlit environment tab filtering works correctly
steps: Step 1: Create 2 dev endpoints and 2 production endpoints
Step 2: Navigate to Streamlit dashboard
Step 3: Click 'Dev' tab using puppeteer_click
Step 4: Verify only 2 dev endpoints are displayed in grid
Step 5: Click 'Production' tab
Step 6: Verify only 2 production endpoints are displayed

description: Streamlit response time chart displays 24h trend with Plotly
steps: Step 1: Create endpoint with 50 check results over last 24h
Step 2: Navigate to Streamlit dashboard
Step 3: Use puppeteer_screenshot to capture chart area
Step 4: Verify Plotly line chart is rendered (check for plotly class elements)
Step 5: Verify red dashed threshold line is visible at configured max response time

description: Streamlit incident log panel shows open and recent incidents
steps: Step 1: Create 2 open incidents and 3 resolved incidents
Step 2: Navigate to Streamlit dashboard
Step 3: Verify incident log panel is visible on right side
Step 4: Verify 2 open incidents are displayed with severity badges
Step 5: Verify last 5 resolved incidents are shown
Step 6: Click 'View Claude Report' expander and verify AI report text is visible

description: Streamlit endpoint detail sidebar shows SLA metrics and check history
steps: Step 1: Create endpoint with check history
Step 2: Navigate to Streamlit dashboard
Step 3: Click endpoint card to open detail sidebar using puppeteer_click
Step 4: Verify SLA metrics section displays 24h/7d/30d uptime percentages
Step 5: Verify check history table is rendered with pagination
Step 6: Verify alert configuration form is visible with channel/target fields

description: Streamlit bulk check now button triggers immediate checks for all endpoints in environment
steps: Step 1: Create 3 endpoints in 'staging' environment
Step 2: Navigate to Streamlit dashboard and select 'Staging' tab
Step 3: Click 'Bulk Check Now' button using puppeteer_click
Step 4: Wait 5 seconds
Step 5: Query checks table and verify 3 new check rows were created for staging endpoints
Step 6: Verify all checks have recent timestamps (within last 10 seconds)

description: Comprehensive end-to-end workflow: add endpoint, detect failure, generate Claude report, send alerts, resolve incident
steps: Step 1: Navigate to Streamlit dashboard at http://localhost:8501
Step 2: Add new endpoint via UI form (or REST API): name='Test API', url='https://httpbin.org/status/503', method='GET'
Step 3: Configure alert settings: email=test@example.com, on_incident=true
Step 4: Trigger 3 manual checks to force failure sequence
Step 5: Verify incident is opened in database with severity and started_at
Step 6: Wait for Claude AI report generation (up to 15 seconds)
Step 7: Verify email alert was sent and recorded in alert log
Step 8: Check incident log panel in Streamlit UI shows new incident with severity badge
Step 9: Click 'View Claude Report' and verify AI analysis is displayed
Step 10: Update endpoint URL to https://httpbin.org/status/200
Step 11: Trigger 2 passing checks
Step 12: Verify incident is auto-closed with resolved_at and duration_mins calculated
Step 13: Verify resolved alert was sent
Step 14: Verify incident moves from 'Open' to 'Resolved' section in Streamlit UI

description: Status card uses correct color-coding: green border for UP, yellow for degraded, red for down
steps: Step 1: Create 3 endpoints: one passing (UP), one degraded (slow response), one failing (DOWN)
Step 2: Navigate to Streamlit dashboard
Step 3: Take screenshot with puppeteer_screenshot
Step 4: Verify passing endpoint card has green (#22C55E) border or background tint
Step 5: Verify degraded endpoint card has amber (#F59E0B) styling
Step 6: Verify failing endpoint card has red (#EF4444) styling

description: Severity badges display correct colors: blue for LOW, orange for MEDIUM, red for HIGH
steps: Step 1: Create 3 incidents with different severities (LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH)
Step 2: Navigate to Streamlit dashboard incident log panel
Step 3: Take screenshot of incident cards
Step 4: Verify LOW severity badge is blue (#3B82F6)
Step 5: Verify MEDIUM severity badge is orange (#F97316)
Step 6: Verify HIGH severity badge is red (#EF4444)

description: Response time chart displays red dashed threshold line correctly
steps: Step 1: Create endpoint with max response time threshold of 1500ms
Step 2: Add check results with response times ranging 500-2500ms
Step 3: Navigate to Streamlit dashboard
Step 4: Take screenshot of response time chart
Step 5: Verify red dashed horizontal line appears at 1500ms mark on Y-axis
Step 6: Verify line color is #EF4444 and style is dashed

description: Streamlit dashboard auto-refreshes every 60 seconds without user interaction
steps: Step 1: Navigate to Streamlit dashboard
Step 2: Add new endpoint via REST API (not through UI)
Step 3: Wait 65 seconds without interacting with browser
Step 4: Verify new endpoint appears in dashboard grid (auto-refresh occurred)
Step 5: Verify page did not do full reload (check browser console for st.rerun)

description: Endpoint detail sidebar displays st.metric format for SLA uptime values
steps: Step 1: Create endpoint with SLA data
Step 2: Navigate to Streamlit dashboard and open endpoint detail sidebar
Step 3: Take screenshot of SLA metrics section
Step 4: Verify uptime percentages are displayed in large bold font (st.metric style)
Step 5: Verify labels '24h Uptime', '7d Uptime', '30d Uptime' are shown in small font above values

description: Keyword validation in response body works correctly
steps: Step 1: Create endpoint with keyword_check='success' pointing to https://httpbin.org/json
Step 2: Trigger manual check
Step 3: Verify check passes if response body contains 'success'
Step 4: Update endpoint to keyword_check='nonexistent_keyword'
Step 5: Trigger check and verify it fails with error_message mentioning missing keyword

description: Concurrent async checks for 10+ endpoints execute without blocking
steps: Step 1: Create 15 endpoints with check_interval=60 seconds
Step 2: Start APScheduler and wait for first check cycle
Step 3: Verify all 15 endpoints have check results within 5 seconds of each other (concurrent execution)
Step 4: Verify total execution time is <10 seconds for all 15 checks (not sequential)
Step 5: Verify no hanging httpx connections in logs

description: FastAPI and Streamlit both access SQLite database without conflicts
steps: Step 1: Start both FastAPI (port 8000) and Streamlit (port 8501) services
Step 2: Create endpoint via FastAPI POST /endpoints
Step 3: Immediately navigate to Streamlit dashboard
Step 4: Verify new endpoint appears in Streamlit grid (no DB lock errors)
Step 5: Trigger check via Streamlit while FastAPI scheduler is running
Step 6: Verify both processes can read/write database concurrently

description: APScheduler jobs reload from database after FastAPI restart
steps: Step 1: Create 3 enabled endpoints with different check intervals
Step 2: Start FastAPI service and verify APScheduler loads all 3 jobs
Step 3: Stop FastAPI service
Step 4: Restart FastAPI service
Step 5: Verify all 3 jobs are re-added to APScheduler from database
Step 6: Verify checks continue executing at configured intervals

description: Disabled endpoints are not scheduled for health checks
steps: Step 1: Create endpoint with enabled=false
Step 2: Wait for 2 check intervals
Step 3: Query checks table and verify no checks were executed for disabled endpoint
Step 4: Enable endpoint via PUT /endpoints/:id/toggle
Step 5: Verify APScheduler adds job and checks start executing

description: Export incident report as PDF via GET /incidents/:id/export/pdf
steps: Step 1: Create endpoint and trigger 3 failures to open an incident
Step 2: Wait up to 15 seconds for Claude AI report to be generated (incidents.claude_report populated)
Step 3: Use puppeteer_evaluate to make fetch() GET to /incidents/:id/export/pdf
Step 4: Verify response status is 200 OK
Step 5: Verify response Content-Type header is application/pdf
Step 6: Verify response Content-Disposition header contains filename ending in .pdf
Step 7: Verify response body byte length is greater than 1024 (valid PDF has content)
Step 8: Verify the PDF bytes start with %PDF- (valid PDF magic bytes, check via base64 decode first 5 chars)
Step 9: Verify PDF contains incident metadata: endpoint name, started_at, severity, failure_count
Step 10: Verify PDF contains Claude AI report text if claude_report field is populated
